name: victorhahn-skills
description: Victor Hahn's personal Claude Code skills and plugins — each installable individually.

plugins.name: aws-solutions-architect
plugins.description: Expert AWS solutions architect — service selection, architecture patterns, cost optimization, and Terraform AWS provider implementation. Use for any AWS question, infrastructure design, or terraform-aws module work.

plugins.name: brainstorming
plugins.description: Turn vague ideas into approved designs through one-question-at-a-time dialogue before any code is written.

plugins.name: cli-creator
plugins.description: Build durable, installable Node/TypeScript CLIs from API docs, OpenAPI specs, curl examples, or existing scripts. Produces tools with stable JSON output, clean auth, and composable commands.

plugins.name: codebase-improvement-audit
plugins.description: Audit a codebase for real improvement opportunities — security, compliance, modernization, dead code, test gaps, dep drift — and produce a prioritized, regression-safe iteration plan.

plugins.name: contract-first-backend
plugins.description: Bootstrap and work with contract-first TypeScript/Express backends where openapi.yaml is the source of truth and all types, validation, and clients are generated.

plugins.name: kubernetes-specialist
plugins.description: Deploy and manage Kubernetes workloads — manifests, RBAC, NetworkPolicies, Helm, debugging, right-sizing, and GitOps.

plugins.name: repo-synthesize
plugins.description: Deep-read one or more repos and produce structured Markdown docs covering both structure and capabilities. Runs a Recon → Delta Plan → Write workflow with an approval gate.

plugins.name: skill-use-audit
plugins.description: Audit how well an installed skill performed in the current Claude Code session — reads the live transcript, finds every invocation, evaluates effectiveness, and proposes diff-style improvements to SKILL.md.

plugins.name: subagent-architect
plugins.description: Design Claude Code subagents and multi-agent workflows — when a subagent is the right tool, what shape it should take, and how it fits the rest of the workflow.

plugins.name: test-coverage-quality-audit
plugins.description: Audit a codebase for test coverage quality — tautological assertions, snapshot abuse, over-mocking, and framework anti-patterns in JS/TS and Go.

plugins.name: tui-creator
plugins.description: Design and implement interactive terminal UIs — layout, color, focus, keybindings, accessibility, and real-world precedent. Framework-agnostic plus Ink 7 / React 19 recipes.

plugins.name: typescript-expert
plugins.description: TypeScript and JavaScript expert — type-level programming, performance, monorepos, migrations, and modern tooling.

plugins.name: unfuck
plugins.description: Make a codebase measurably better through small, surgical, regression-proof changes — coverage adds, dead-code removal, control-flow tightening, safe renames. Cardinal rule: no confidence, no change.

plugins.name: visualize
plugins.description: Produce rendered Mermaid diagrams and self-contained HTML visuals when the answer should be a diagram, not prose.

plugins.name: gh-org-context
plugins.description: Distill a GitHub org (or curated repo list) into dense, structured context — tech stack, domains, runbooks, cross-repo connections — so downstream skills have an onboarded-engineer mental model. Bundles slash commands and the org-context skill.
